motion.name: ease-expressive
motion.purpose: Default easing for the hero's reveal motion.

motion.name: stagger-hero
motion.purpose: Offset between nav, copy, image, and supporting rows.

motion.name: delay-hero
motion.purpose: Initial delay before the first hero group appears.

elevationMeta.strategy: Flat by default. Depth comes from borders, tone, rounded corners, crop, and overlap.
notes: Do not introduce ambient shadow stacks.
The availability card should feel placed, not floated.

display.purpose: Hero headline only, the emotional anchor.
display.fontFamily: Iowan Old Style, Baskerville, Palatino Linotype, Georgia, serif

headline.purpose: Small emphatic labels, action text, and compact hero lines.
headline.fontFamily: Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Segoe UI, system-ui, sans-serif

body.fontFamily: Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Segoe UI, system-ui, sans-serif

label.purpose: Trust row labels, stat labels, and other metadata.
label.fontFamily: Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Segoe UI, system-ui, sans-serif

eyebrow.purpose: The tiny studio line above the hero headline.
eyebrow.fontFamily: Avenir Next, Helvetica Neue, Segoe UI, system-ui, sans-serif

page-shell.description: One-screen desktop shell with max-width 1440px and responsive padding.
page-shell.behavior: Locks the composition into a single hero view on large screens.

button-primary.description: Primary pill CTA used in the hero and header.
button-primary.behavior: Small, dark, and tactile. Hover lifts slightly.

button-secondary.description: Secondary text action with a quiet underline.
button-secondary.behavior: Underline reads as a link, not a button banner.

nav-link.description: Minimal header link in small tracked text.
nav-link.behavior: Muted by default, darker on hover.

hero-image.description: Rounded portrait frame with a 7:8 crop.

status-card.description: Floating availability note that overlaps the portrait.
status-card.behavior: Placed against the lower-right edge rather than floated with shadow.

trust-row.description: Single-line proof strip for partner names.

stats-row.description: Capability strip with numbers and brief support copy.
stats-row.behavior: Four compact columns on desktop, stacked on smaller screens.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main hero CTA and header CTA.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 0.5rem; border: 0; border-radius: 9999px; padding: 0.625rem 1.25rem; background: var(--fg); color: var(--bg); font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; text-decoration: none; transition: transform 300ms ease, background-color 300ms ease, color 300ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--line); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Link
components.description: Text-first secondary action with an underline.
components.css: .ds-link-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line); padding-bottom: 0.25rem; color: var(--muted-strong); font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; text-decoration: none; transition: color 300ms ease, transform 300ms ease, border-color 300ms ease; } .ds-link-secondary:hover { color: var(--fg); transform: translateX(2px); } .ds-link-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--line); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Nav Link
components.css: .ds-nav-link { color: var(--muted-strong); font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1; text-decoration: none; transition: color 300ms ease, transform 300ms ease; } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: var(--fg); } .ds-nav-link:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--line); outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Hero Image Frame
components.description: Rounded portrait frame for the main hero image.
components.css: .ds-hero-image { width: min(28rem, 100%); margin: 0; border: 1px solid var(--line); border-radius: 32px; overflow: hidden; background: var(--panel); } .ds-hero-image img { display: block; width: 100%; aspect-ratio: 7 / 8; object-fit: cover; object-position: 62% center; }

components.name: Availability Card
components.description: Small overlapping status note placed against the portrait.
components.html:
Open for select projects.

Replies within one business day.
components.css: .ds-status-card { width: min(15rem, 100%); border: 1px solid var(--line); border-radius: 19px; background: var(--panel); padding: 0.875rem 1rem; color: var(--fg); } .ds-status-chip { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; } .ds-status-dot { width: 0.5rem; height: 0.5rem; border-radius: 9999px; background: var(--muted-strong); } .ds-status-label { font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.1em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); } .ds-status-card p { margin: 0.5rem 0 0; font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; color: var(--muted-strong); }

components.name: Trust Row
components.description: Quiet logo strip that sits below the hero.
components.css: .ds-trust-row { border-top: 1px solid var(--line); padding-top: 1.5rem; display: grid; gap: 1.25rem; } .ds-trust-row p { margin: 0; font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.24em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); } .ds-trust-row ul { margin: 0; padding: 0; list-style: none; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.75rem 2rem; font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 0.78rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.14em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted-strong); }

components.name: Stats Row
components.description: Four-column capability strip with numbers and support copy.
components.html:
03Disciplines

Strategy, interface design, and front-end build.

12Launches

Portfolio sites, product pages, and brand moments.

24hReply time

Fast responses for new project inquiries.

24hReply time

Fast responses for new project inquiries.
components.css: .ds-stats-row { border-top: 1px solid var(--line); padding-top: 1.25rem; display: grid; gap: 1.25rem; } .ds-stat { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.375rem; } .ds-stat-header { display: flex; align-items: end; gap: 0.75rem; } .ds-stat-value { font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 1.8rem; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1; letter-spacing: -0.05em; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; color: var(--fg); } .ds-stat-label { font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 0.68rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.24em; text-transform: uppercase; color: var(--muted); } .ds-stat-detail { max-width: 22ch; margin: 0; font-family: var(--font-sans); font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.45; text-align: center; color: var(--muted-strong); } @media (min-width: 768px) { .ds-stats-row { grid-template-columns: repeat(4, minmax(0, 1fr)); } }

narrative.overview:
This system frames the portfolio like a museum label beside a large portrait, calm, specific, and expensive without trying too hard. The left column carries the voice, the right column carries the image, and the lower rows provide proof without competing with the hero. On desktop the whole composition is meant to read at a glance inside a `100svh` viewport, so the page feels complete before any scroll.

The visual language is minimal, editorial, and warm. It rejects startup clutter, generic AI SaaS layouts, loud gradients, glassmorphism, heavy shadows, neon accents, and anything that makes the page feel like a template trying to perform sophistication. Structure comes from typography, crop, overlap, and measured whitespace, not decoration.
keyCharacteristics: warm paper background, charcoal text, linen borders
serif display type paired with a grounded sans body face
left text column, right portrait column, floating status card
flat surfaces with overlap instead of shadow theatrics
compact support rows for trust and stats, never noisy card grids
full hero visibility on desktop, looser stack on mobile

rules.name: The One Ink Rule
rules.body: Keep the system in warm neutrals and a single dark ink tone. If a new accent cannot justify itself as paper, linen, taupe, or ink, it does not belong.

rules.name: The Mixed-Line Rule
rules.body: The hero can mix sans and serif inside the same headline, but the contrast should stay restrained. One line of sans, one or two lines of serif, then stop.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Use borders and overlap before shadow. If a surface needs more hierarchy, change the layout or the tone, not the blur.

dos: Do keep the desktop shell inside `100svh` and `overflow-hidden` so the full hero is visible at one glance.
Do keep the headline compact, around `12ch`, with the body at `34ch`.
Do keep the image around `28rem` wide with a `7:8` crop and the floating card around `15rem` wide.
Do use the existing warm neutral palette, with the page background, image frame, and support surfaces staying close in value.
Do let the left column and right column feel intentionally asymmetrical, but never disconnected.
donts: Don't introduce gradients, glassmorphism, glowing effects, or heavy shadows.
Don't turn the hero into a generic centered SaaS stack or a startup-style cluttered landing page.
Don't let body copy run wide, the type should stay calm and readable, not stretched across the screen.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than `1px` as a colored accent.
Don't add extra accent colors, random floating shapes, or identical card grids that make the page feel templated.
Don't push the image so tall that the hero stops reading as one composition.
